records.ety: [L. syntaxis, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to put together in order; sy`n with + <?/ to put in order; cf. F. syntaxe. See Syn-, and Tactics.]

defs: Connected system or order; union of things; a number of things jointed together; organism.
quotes: They owe no other dependence to the first than what is common to the whole syntax of beings.

defs: That part of grammar which treats of the construction of sentences; the due arrangement of words in sentences in their necessary relations, according to established usage in any language.
